pattern: The dash count before each value is exactly the node's depth, so a stack of the current ancestor chain tells you precisely where to attach the next node.
hints: Parse the string into (depth, value) pairs by counting leading dashes before each number.
Maintain a stack representing the path from the root to the most recently placed node; its size equals the current depth + 1.
Pop the stack until its size matches the new node's depth, then attach the new node as the left child if that slot is free, otherwise the right child, and push it.

edge_inputs.description: full binary tree across three depths

stress.description: build a random tree obeying the left-child-first rule, then serialize to the dash format
stress.note: serialization preserves the left-before-right preorder format the problem expects
